blocks:
Surah Al-Mujadalah

Medinan. Its number of verses is twenty-one verses (according to) the Makkan and the Last Medinan (counts), and two verses (more) according to the rest (of the counts). Its difference is one verse in al-Adhallīn (the least common counts) other than the Makkan and the Last Medinan (counts).* بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ*** (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) قَدْ سَمِعَ اللهُ قَوْلَ الَّتِي تُجادِلُكَ فِي زَوْجِها وَتَشْتَكِي إِلَى اللهِ وَاللهُ يَسْمَعُ تَحاوُرَكُما ** Your mutual exchange of speech (تراجعكما الكلام) إِنَّ اللهَ سَمِيعٌ بَصِيرٌ ** Of the sayings (الأقوال) and the states (الأحوال). (1) Allah has certainly heard the speech of her who pleaded with you concerning her husband and complained to Allah. And Allah hears your dialogue. Indeed Allah is all-hearing, all-seeing.

(٢) الَّذِينَ يُظاهِرُونَ مِنْكُمْ مِنْ نِسائِهِمْ ** الظهّار is when a man says to his wife, "You are to me like the back (ظهر) of my mother." It is derived (مشتقّ) from الظهر (the back). And it was recited (وقرئ) يظهرون (from أظهر) and يظاهرون (from ظاهر). ما هُنَّ أُمَّهاتِهِمْ ** In reality (على الحقيقة). إِنْ أُمَّهاتُهُمْ إِلَّا اللَّائِي وَلَدْنَهُمْ وَإِنَّهُمْ لَيَقُولُونَ مُنْكَراً مِنَ الْقَوْلِ وَزُوراً وَإِنَّ اللهَ لَعَفُوٌّ غَفُورٌ ** For what has passed from him. (2) As for those of you who pronounce ẓihār on their wives, they are not their mothers. Their mothers are no one but those who bore them, and indeed they say an objectionable word and a falsehood. And indeed Allah is all-pardoning, all-forgiving.**

(٣) وَالَّذِينَ يُظاهِرُونَ مِنْ نِسائِهِمْ ثُمَّ يَعُودُونَ لِما قالُوا ** (3) Those who pronounce ẓihār on their wives, and then retract what they have said, It was said: meaning, returning to their saying (قولهم) by rectifying (بالتدارك) through the annulment (بنقض) of what it necessitates (ما يقتضيه). Another interpretation (تفسير آخر) for it will come soon. فَتَحْرِيرُ رَقَبَةٍ مِنْ قَبْلِ أَنْ يَتَمَاسَّا ذلِكُمْ تُوعَظُونَ بِهِ ** must emancipate a slave before they touch each other. This is what you are advised to do, so that you may be deterred (ترتدعوا) from the like of it. **وَاللهُ بِما تَعْمَلُونَ خَبِيرٌ ** and Allah is well aware of what you do. no hidden thing is concealed from Him.

(٤) فَمَنْ لَمْ يَجِدْ الرقبة فَصِيامُ شَهْرَيْنِ مُتَتابِعَيْنِ ** (4) But whoever does not find [a slave], let him fast for two successive months بأن يصوم شهراً ومن الآخر شيئاً متّصلاً به ثمّ يتمّ الآخر متوالياً أو متفرّقاً مِنْ قَبْلِ أَنْ يَتَمَاسَّا ** by fasting one month and a portion of the other connected to it, then completing the rest of the second month consecutively or separately, ** مِنْ قَبْلِ أَنْ يَتَمَاسَّا ** (before they touch each other) بالمجامعة فَمَنْ لَمْ يَسْتَطِعْ ** meaning, by intercourse (بالمجامعة). ** فَمَنْ لَمْ يَسْتَطِعْ ** (But whoever is unable) الصيام من مرض أو عطاش أو نحو ذلك فَإِطْعامُ سِتِّينَ مِسْكِيناً to fast, due to illness, intense thirst (عطاش), or the like, ** فَإِطْعامُ سِتِّينَ مِسْكِيناً ** (let him feed sixty needy persons) بقدر شبعهم أو إعطاء مدّ لكلّ مسكين ذلِكَ لِتُؤْمِنُوا بِاللهِ وَرَسُولِهِ ** to the extent of their fill, or by giving a mudd (مدّ) to each needy person. ** ذلِكَ لِتُؤْمِنُوا بِاللهِ وَرَسُولِهِ ** (That is so that you may believe in Allah and His Apostle). فرض ذلك لتصدّقوا بالله ورسوله في قبول شرايعه ورفض ما كنتم عليه في جاهليّتكم He imposed that so that you may affirm Allah and His Apostle by accepting His laws (شرايعه) and rejecting what you were upon during your time of ignorance (جاهليّتكم). وَتِلْكَ حُدُودُ اللهِ ** ** وَتِلْكَ حُدُودُ اللهِ ** (These are Allah’s bounds) لا يجوز تعدّيها وَلِلْكافِرِينَ ** which it is not permissible to transgress. ** وَلِلْكافِرِينَ ** (And there is a painful punishment for the faithless) الذين لا يقبلونها عَذابٌ أَلِيمٌ those who do not accept them. ** عَذابٌ أَلِيمٌ ** (a painful punishment). القمّيّ قال : كان سبب نزول هذه الآية انّه أوّل من ظاهر في الإسلام كان رجلاً يقال له أوس بن الصامت بن الأنصار وكان شيخاً كبيراً فغضب على اهله يوماً فقال لها أنت عَلَيَّ كظهر امّي ثمّ ندم على ذلك قال وكان الرجل في الجاهليّة إذا قال لأهله أنت عليّ كظهر امّي حرمت عليه آخر الأبد وقال أوس لأهله يا خولة انّا كنّا نحرّم هذا في الجاهليّة وقد أتانا الله بالإسلام فاذهبي الى رسول الله صلى الله عليه وآله فاسألي عن ذلك فأتت خولة رسول الله صلى الله عليه وآله فقالت بأبي أنت وأمّي يا رسول الله انّ أوس بن الصامت هو زوجي وأبو ولدي وابن عمّي فقال لي أنت عليّ كظهر امّي وانّا نحرّم ذلك في الجاهليّة وقد أتانا الله بالإسلام بك. Al-Qummi said: The reason for the revelation of this verse was that the first person to commit zihar (ظاهر) in Islam was a man called Aws ibn al-Samit al-Ansari (أوس بن الصامت بن الأنصار). He was an old man, and one day he became angry with his wife and said to her: "You are to me like the back of my mother (أنت عَلَيَّ كظهر امّي)." Then he regretted that. He said: In the time of ignorance (الجاهليّة), when a man said to his wife, "You are to me like the back of my mother," she became forbidden to him forever. Aws said to his wife, Khawlah (خولة): "O Khawlah, we used to forbid this in the time of ignorance, but Allah has brought us Islam. Go to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله and ask about this." So Khawlah came to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله and said: "May my father and mother be sacrificed for you, O Messenger of Allah, Aws ibn al-Samit is my husband, the father of my children, and my cousin. He said to me, 'You are to me like the back of my mother,' and we used to forbid that in the time of ignorance, but Allah has brought us Islam through you." وفي الفقيه عن الصادق عليه السلام ما في معناه وزاد في آخره : فقال لها رسول الله صلى الله عليه وآله ايّتها المرأة ما اظنّك إلّا وقد حرمت عليه فرفعت المرأة يدها إلى السماء فقالت أشكو إلى الله فراق زوجي فأنزل الله يا محمّد قَدْ سَمِعَ اللهُ ** In al-Faqih, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام, is a narration similar in meaning, with the addition at the end: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said to her: "O woman, I do not think but that you have become forbidden to him." So the woman raised her hands to the sky and said: "I complain to Allah about the separation from my husband." Then Allah revealed, "O Muhammad, ** قَدْ سَمِعَ اللهُ ** (Allah has certainly heard)" إلى قوله لَعَفُوٌّ غَفُورٌ ** up to His saying ** لَعَفُوٌّ غَفُورٌ ** (All-forgiving, All-merciful). قال ثمّ أنزل الله الكفّارة في ذلك فقال وَالَّذِينَ يُظاهِرُونَ مِنْ نِسائِهِمْ ** He said: Then Allah revealed the expiation (الكفّارة) concerning that, and He said: ** وَالَّذِينَ يُظاهِرُونَ مِنْ نِسائِهِمْ ** (Those who pronounce zihar against their wives) الى عَذابٌ أَلِيمٌ. up to ** عَذابٌ أَلِيمٌ ** (a painful punishment). وفي الكافي عن الباقر عليه السلام انّ أمير المؤمنين عليه السلام قال : انّ امرأة من المسلمات أتت النبيّ صلى الله عليه وآله فقالت يا رسول الله انّ فلاناً زوجي وقد نثرت له بطني وأعنته على دنياه وآخرته لم يرمّني مكروهاً أشكوه إلى الله وإليك فقال ممّا تشكينه فقالت انّه قال أنت عليّ حرام كظهر امّي وقد اخرجني من منزلي فانظر في أمري فقال لها رسول الله صلى الله عليه وآله ما أنزل الله تبارك وتعالى كتاباً أقضي فيه بينك وبين زوجك وانا اكره ان أكون من المتكلّفين فجعلت تبكي وتشتكي ما بها إلى الله عزّ وجلّ وإلى رسول الله صلى الله عليه وآله وانصرفت قال فسمع الله تبارك وتعالى مجادلتها لرسول الله صلى الله عليه وآله في زوجها وما شكت إليه فأنزل الله عزّ وجلّ في ذلك قُرآناً بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ قَدْ سَمِعَ اللهُ قَوْلَ الَّتِي تُجادِلُكَ فِي زَوْجِها وَتَشْتَكِي إِلَى اللهِ وَاللهُ يَسْمَعُ تَحاوُرُكُما ** In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir عليه السلام, the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام said: A Muslim woman came to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله and said: "O Messenger of Allah, so-and-so is my husband. I have borne him children (نثرت له بطني) and I have helped him in his worldly life and his Hereafter. He has not afflicted me with anything disliked (مكروهاً). I complain about him to Allah and to you." He asked: "What do you complain about?" She replied: "He said, 'You are forbidden to me like the back of my mother (أنت عَلَيَّ حرام كظهر امّي),' and he has expelled me from my house. So look into my affair." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said to her: "Allah, the Blessed and Almighty, has not revealed a Book by which I may judge between you and your husband, and I dislike being one of those who impose burdens (المتكلّفين)." So she began to weep and complain about her situation to Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, and to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله, and she departed. He said: Then Allah, the Blessed and Almighty, heard her arguing (مجادلتها) with the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله concerning her husband and what she complained about to Him. So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed a Quranic verse concerning that: ** بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ قَدْ سَمِعَ اللهُ قَوْلَ الَّتِي تُجادِلُكَ فِي زَوْجِها وَتَشْتَكِي إِلَى اللهِ وَاللهُ يَسْمَعُ تَحاوُرُكُما ** (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful. Allah has certainly heard the speech of her who pleads with you concerning her husband and complains to Allah, and Allah hears your conversation). يعني محاورتها لرسول الله صلى الله عليه وآله في زوجها إِنَّ اللهَ سَمِيعٌ بَصِيرٌ الَّذِينَ يُظاهِرُونَ مِنْكُمْ ** Meaning, her dialogue (محاورتها) with the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله concerning her husband. ** إِنَّ اللهَ سَمِيعٌ بَصِيرٌ الَّذِينَ يُظاهِرُونَ مِنْكُمْ ** (Indeed Allah is all-hearing, all-seeing. Those of you who pronounce zihar against their wives) الآية قال فبعث رسول الله صلى الله عليه وآله الى المرأة فأتته فقال لها جيئيني بزوجك فأتت به فقال أقلت لامرأتك هذه أنت عَلَيَّ حرام كظهر امّي فقال قد قلت لها ذاك فقال له رسول الله صلى الله عليه وآله قد أنزل الله تبارك وتعالى فيك وفي امرأتك قرآناً فقرأ عليه ما انزل الله قَدْ سَمِعَ اللهُ ** the verse. He said: Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله sent for the woman, and she came to Him. He said to her: "Bring me your husband." So she brought him. He asked: "Did you say to this wife of yours, 'You are forbidden to me like the back of my mother'?" He replied: "I did say that to her." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said to him: "Allah, the Blessed and Almighty, has revealed a Quranic verse concerning you and your wife." Then He recited to him what Allah had revealed: ** قَدْ سَمِعَ اللهُ ** (Allah has certainly heard) إلى قوله لَعَفُوٌّ غَفُورٌ up to His saying ** لَعَفُوٌّ غَفُورٌ ** (All-forgiving, All-merciful). ثم قال فضمّ إليك امرأتك فانّك قد قلت منكراً من القول وزوراً وقد عفا الله عنك وغفر لك ولا تعد قال فانصرف الرجل وهو نادم على ما قاله لامرأته وكره الله عزّ وجلّ ذلك للمؤمنين بعد وأنزل الله الَّذِينَ يُظاهِرُونَ مِنْ نِسائِهِمْ ثُمَّ يَعُودُونَ لِما قالُوا ** Then He said: "Take your wife back to yourself, for you have certainly spoken an abominable word (منكراً من القول) and a falsehood (زوراً). Allah has pardoned you and forgiven you, so do not repeat it." He said: The man departed, regretting what he had said to his wife. And Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, disliked that for the believers thereafter, and Allah revealed: ** الَّذِينَ يُظاهِرُونَ مِنْ نِسائِهِمْ ثُمَّ يَعُودُونَ لِما قالُوا ** (Those who pronounce zihar against their wives and then retract what they have said). قال يعني ما قال الرجل الأول لامرأته أنت عليّ كظهر امّي قال فمن قالها بعد ما عفا الله وغفر للرجل الأوّل فانّ عَلَيْهِ فَتَحْرِيرُ رَقَبَةٍ مِنْ قَبْلِ أَنْ يَتَمَاسَّا يعني مجامعتها ذلِكُمْ تُوعَظُونَ بِهِ وَاللهُ بِما تَعْمَلُونَ خَبِيرٌ He said: Meaning what the first man said to his wife, "You are to me like the back of my mother." He said: So whoever says it after Allah pardoned and forgave the first man, then ** فَتَحْرِيرُ رَقَبَةٍ مِنْ قَبْلِ أَنْ يَتَمَاسَّا ** (then [the penalty is] the freeing of a slave before they touch each other), meaning, having intercourse (مجامعتها). ** ذلِكُمْ تُوعَظُونَ بِهِ وَاللهُ بِما تَعْمَلُونَ خَبِيرٌ ** (That is what you are exhorted to do, and Allah is well aware of what you do). قال فَمَنْ لَمْ يَجِدْ فَصِيامُ شَهْرَيْنِ مُتَتابِعَيْنِ ** He said: ** فَمَنْ لَمْ يَجِدْ فَصِيامُ شَهْرَيْنِ مُتَتابِعَيْنِ ** (But whoever does not find [a slave], let him fast for two successive months), يعني مِنْ قَبْلِ أَنْ يَتَمَاسَّا فَمَنْ لَمْ يَسْتَطِعْ فَإِطْعامُ سِتِّينَ مِسْكِيناً meaning ** مِنْ قَبْلِ أَنْ يَتَمَاسَّا ** (before they touch each other). ** فَمَنْ لَمْ يَسْتَطِعْ فَإِطْعامُ سِتِّينَ مِسْكِيناً ** (But whoever is unable, let him feed sixty needy persons). قال فجعل الله عقوبة من ظاهر بعد النهي هذا ثمّ قال ذلِكَ لِتُؤْمِنُوا بِاللهِ وَرَسُولِهِ وَتِلْكَ حُدُودُ اللهِ قال هذا حدّ الظهّار ثمّ قال عليه السلام ولا يكون ظهار في يمين ولا في إضرار ولا في غضب ولا يكون ظهار الّا على طهر من غير جماع بشهادة شاهدين مسلمين. He said: Thus Allah made this the punishment for whoever commits zihar (ظاهر) after the prohibition. Then He said: ** ذلِكَ لِتُؤْمِنُوا بِاللهِ وَرَسُولِهِ وَتِلْكَ حُدُودُ اللهِ ** (That is so that you may believe in Allah and His Apostle. These are Allah’s bounds). He said: This is the boundary (حدّ) of zihar (الظهّار). Then he عليه السلام said: Zihar (ظهار) does not occur in an oath (يمين), nor in causing harm (إضرار), nor in anger (غضب). And zihar does not occur except during a state of purity (طهر) without intercourse (جماع), with the testimony of two Muslim witnesses (شاهدين مسلمين). والقمّيّ عن الباقر عليه السلام قال : ان امرأة الحديث بأدنى تفاوت في ألفاظه. And al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir عليه السلام, said: The narration of the woman is with the slightest variation in its wording (ألفاظه). وفي الكافي عن الصادق عليه السلام : أنّه سئل عن رجل مملك (١) ظاهر من امرأته قال لا يكون ظهار ولا إيلاء حتّى يدخل بها وتفاصيل احكام الظهار تطلب من كتب الأخبار. In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام: He was asked about a man who owned a slave-woman (مملك) (1) and committed zihar against her. He said: Zihar (ظهار) and ila' (إيلاء) do not occur until he has consummated the marriage with her (يدخل بها). The details of the rulings of zihar (الظهار) should be sought from the books of narrations (كتب الأخبار).

(٥) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يُحَادُّونَ اللهَ وَرَسُولَهُ ** (5) Indeed those who oppose Allah and His Apostle they show enmity to both of them, for each of the two adversaries is in a boundary (حَدّ) other than the boundary (حَدّ) of the other. And it was said: they establish boundaries (حُدُوداً) other than His boundaries (حُدُودَهُما). كُبِتُوا ** they were repelled and destroyed, and the origin (أَصْل) of الكَبْت is الكَبّ. كَما كُبِتَ الَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِهِمْ ** (shall be disgraced, just as those who were before them were disgraced) meaning the disbelievers (كُفَّار) of past nations (الأُمَم المَاضِيَة). وَقَدْ أَنْزَلْنا آياتٍ بَيِّناتٍ ** (We have certainly sent down manifest signs) which indicate the truthfulness of the Apostle and what he brought. وَلِلْكافِرِينَ عَذابٌ مُهِينٌ (and there is a humiliating punishment for the faithless) which removes their glory (عِزّ) and their arrogance (تَكَبُّر).

(٦)** ** يَوْمَ يَبْعَثُهُمُ اللهُ جَمِيعاً ** (6) The day Allah raises them all all of them, leaving none, or gathered together.

فَيُنَبِّئُهُمْ بِما عَمِلُوا and informs them about what they have done. meaning, in the presence of the witnesses (عَلَى رُؤُوسِ الْأَشْهَادِ), as an affirmation of their punishment.

**أَحْصاهُ اللهُ ** Allah has enumerated it, He encompassed it by number, such that nothing escaped Him.

**وَنَسُوهُ ** while they have forgotten it. due to its abundance or their negligence concerning it.

**وَاللهُ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ شَهِيدٌ ** Allah is witness to all things. Nothing is absent from Him.

footnote (١) الاملاك : التزويج في عقد النكاح. (1) Al-Imlāk (الْإِمْلَاك): Marriage (التَّزْوِيج) in the marriage contract (عَقْدِ النِّكَاح).

(٧) أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّ اللهَ يَعْلَمُ ما فِي السَّماواتِ وَما فِي الْأَرْضِ ما يَكُونُ مِنْ نَجْوى ثَلاثَةٍ

(7) Have you not regarded that Allah knows whatever is in the heavens and whatever is in the earth? There is no secret counsel of three (meaning) of three who hold a secret counsel, or of three who are holding a secret counsel, إِلَّا هُوَ رابِعُهُمْ ** but He is their fourth, except that Allah makes them four, since He shares with them in being aware (الاطّلاع) of it (the counsel). وَلا خَمْسَةٍ ** nor of five, nor a secret counsel of five, إِلَّا هُوَ سادِسُهُمْ وَلا أَدْنى مِنْ ذلِكَ وَلا أَكْثَرَ إِلَّا هُوَ مَعَهُمْ ** but He is their sixth, nor of fewer than that, nor of more, but He is with them, knowing what transpires between them, أَيْنَ ما كانُوا ** wherever they may be. For His knowledge of things is not due to spatial proximity (قرب مكانيّ) such that it would vary with the difference in locations (الامكنة).

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He means by encompassing (الإحاطة) and knowledge (العلم), not by the Essence (الذات), because places (الأماكن) are limited, contained by four boundaries (حدود أربعة). If He were there by His Essence (بالذات), containment (الحواية) would be necessitated upon Him.

And: The Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) was asked about Allah, "Where is He?" He replied: "He is here and here, and above and below, and encompassing us and with us." Then He recited this Ayah. He indicated that He is the fourth of the three and the sixth of the five who hold secret counsel only through His encompassing (باحاطته) them, His dominance (غلبته) over them, His knowledge of what they counsel secretly, and His witnessing (شهوده) their secret counsel in their presence—not that He is one of them or counted among them by His Holy Essence (بذاته المقدّسة), because that would necessitate limitation (الحدّ), place (المكان), and containment (الحواية). ثُمَّ يُنَبِّئُهُمْ بِما عَمِلُوا يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ ** Then He will inform them of what they have done on the Day of Resurrection, as an affirmation of the recompense (الجزاء) they deserve. إِنَّ اللهَ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ عَلِيمٌ ** Indeed Allah has knowledge of all things. Nothing hidden is concealed from Him.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): This Ayah was revealed concerning so-and-so (فلان), and so-and-so (فلان), and Abu Ubaydah ibn al-Jarrah, and Abd al-Rahman ibn Awf, and Salim, the freed slave (مولى) of Abu Hudhayfah, and al-Mughirah ibn Shu’bah, when they wrote the document (الكتاب) among themselves, and made a covenant (تعاهدوا) and swore oaths (تواثقوا) that if Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله passed away, the Caliphate (الخلافة) would never be among Banu Hashim, nor the Prophethood (النبوّة) ever. And al-Qummi narrated what is similar in meaning.

(٨) أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَى الَّذِينَ نُهُوا عَنِ النَّجْوى ثُمَّ يَعُودُونَ لِما نُهُوا عَنْهُ ** (8) Have you not regarded those who were forbidden secret talks, but then reverted to what they were forbidden? It is said that [this Ayah] was revealed concerning the Jews and the hypocrites. They used to hold secret talks among themselves and wink with their eyes when they saw the believers. Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله forbade them, but they reverted to the like of their action. وَيَتَناجَوْنَ بِالْإِثْمِ وَالْعُدْوانِ وَمَعْصِيَةِ الرَّسُولِ ** (They talk secretly about sin, hostility, and disobedience to the Apostle.) Meaning: with that which is sin (إثم) and hostility (عدوان) towards the believers, and mutual recommendation (تواص) of disobedience to the Messenger. And it was read (وقرئ) as وَيَتَنَجَّوْنَ. A hadith supports this, [which states]: "I did not hold a secret talk (انتجيته) with him, rather Allah held a secret talk (انتجاه) with him," concerning the matter of Ali (peace be upon him). وَإِذا جاؤُكَ حَيَّوْكَ بِما لَمْ يُحَيِّكَ بِهِ اللهُ ** (When they come to you, they greet you with a greeting that Allah has not greeted you with.) So they say: السّام عليك (Death be upon you) or أنعم صباحاً (May your morning be pleasant) and أنعم مساء (May your evening be pleasant). Whereas Allah, the Glorified, says: وَسَلامٌ عَلى عِبادِهِ الَّذِينَ اصْطَفى.** (And peace be upon His servants whom He has chosen.)

In Rawdat al-Wa'izin (روضة الواعظين), it is narrated: The Jews came to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله and said: السّام عليك يا محمّد (Death be upon you, O Muhammad). And السّام in their language means death (الموت). The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله replied: وعليكم (And upon you). Then Allah revealed this Ayah. And al-Qummi (القمّيّ) [narrates]: When they came to Him, they said to Him: أنعم صباحاً (May your morning be pleasant) and أنعم مساء (May your evening be pleasant), which is the greeting of the people of Jahiliyyah (الجاهلية). Then Allah revealed this Ayah. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله then said to them: "Allah has replaced that for us with something better: the greeting of the people of Paradise, السلام عليكم (Peace be upon you)." ** وَيَقُولُونَ فِي أَنْفُسِهِمْ ** (and say in their hearts) Among themselves (فيما بينهم): لَوْ لا يُعَذِّبُنَا اللهُ بِما نَقُولُ ** (Why does not Allah punish us for what we say?) [Meaning]: Why does He not punish us for that, if Muhammad were truly a Prophet? حَسْبُهُمْ جَهَنَّمُ ** (Hell is enough for them) as a punishment (عذاباً). يَصْلَوْنَها ** (where they shall enter) [meaning]: they shall enter it (يدخلونها). An evil destination is Hell (جهنّم).

(٩) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِذا تَناجَيْتُمْ فَلا تَتَناجَوْا بِالْإِثْمِ وَالْعُدْوانِ وَمَعْصِيَةِ الرَّسُولِ ** (9) O you who have faith! When you secretly converse, do not converse secretly about sin, aggression, and disobedience to the Apostle, as the hypocrites (المنافقون) do, وَتَناجَوْا بِالْبِرِّ وَالتَّقْوى ** and converse secretly about piety and God-wariness, with what comprises the welfare of the believers and guarding against disobedience to the Apostle, **وَاتَّقُوا اللهَ الَّذِي إِلَيْهِ تُحْشَرُونَ ** and be wary of Allah toward Whom you will be mustered, regarding what you do and what you leave, for He will recompense you for it.

(١٠) ** إِنَّمَا النَّجْوى مِنَ الشَّيْطانِ ** (10) Secret counsels are only from Satan for he is the one who beautifies it (يُزَيِّنُهَا) and incites towards it, ** لِيَحْزُنَ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا ** (so that he may grieve those who have faith), by their imagining that it concerns a calamity that has afflicted them. ** وَلَيْسَ ** (And neither) Satan nor the secret counseling ** بِضارِّهِمْ ** (is) harming them—meaning, harming the believers— شَيْئاً إِلَّا بِإِذْنِ اللهِ ** (in the least, except by Allah’s leave), meaning, by His will (بِمَشِيَّتِهِ). ** وَعَلَى اللهِ فَلْيَتَوَكَّلِ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ ** (And in Allah let the faithful put their trust), and let them not care about their secret counsel (نَجْوَاهُمْ).

Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him) that He was asked about the saying of Allah: ** إِنَّمَا النَّجْوى مِنَ الشَّيْطانِ ** (Secret counsels are only from Satan). He said: "The second one (الثَّانِي)."

And in al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, He said: "If you are three, two should not hold a secret counsel excluding their companion, for that grieves him." And concerning this verse, it was said that the meaning intended by the Ayah is the dreams (أَحْلَامُ الْمَنَامِ) that a person sees in his sleep, which then grieve him.

And Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The reason for the revelation of this Ayah was that Fatimah (peace be upon her) saw in her sleep that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم intended to leave al-Madinah along with Fatimah, Ali, al-Hasan, and al-Husayn (peace be upon them). So they departed until they passed the walls (حِيطَان) of al-Madinah. Two paths appeared before them, and the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم took the right path until He reached a place containing date palms and water. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم bought a dharrā’ sheep (شَاة ذَرَاء)—which is one that has white spots in one of its ears—and ordered it to be slaughtered. When they ate, they died in their place. Fatimah (peace be upon her) woke up weeping and terrified, but she did not inform the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم of this. When morning came, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم brought a donkey and mounted Fatimah (peace be upon her) upon it, and ordered the Commander of the Faithful (أَمِيرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ), al-Hasan, and al-Husayn (peace be upon them) to leave al-Madinah, just as Fatimah (peace be upon her) had seen in her sleep. When they left the walls of al-Madinah, two paths appeared before them, and the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم took the right path, just as Fatimah (peace be upon her) had seen, until they reached a place containing date palms and water. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم bought a dharrā’ sheep (شَاة ذَرَاء), just as Fatimah (peace be upon her) had seen, and ordered it to be slaughtered. It was slaughtered and roasted. When they intended to eat it, Fatimah (peace be upon her) stood up and withdrew to one side, weeping, fearing that they would die. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم sought her until He found her weeping. He said: "What is the matter with you, O my daughter?" She said: "O Messenger of Allah, last night I saw such-and-such in my sleep, and you have done exactly as I saw, so I withdrew from you so that I would not see you die." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم then prayed two rak'ahs and then held a secret counsel (نَاجَى) with his Lord. Jibril (peace be upon him) descended upon Him and said: "O Muhammad, this is a devil called al-Zahā (الزَّهَا), and he is the one who showed Fatimah this vision (الرُّؤْيَا) and harms the believers in their sleep with things that grieve them." Jibril (peace be upon him) commanded, and the devil was brought to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. He said to him: "Are you the one who showed Fatimah this vision?" He said: "Yes, O Muhammad." So, He spat upon him three ugly spittle (بَزَقَات) in three places. Then Jibril said to Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "O Muhammad, if you see something in your sleep that you dislike, or if any of the believers sees it, let him say: 'I seek refuge in that by which the near-stationed angels of Allah (مَلَائِكَةُ اللهِ الْمُقَرَّبُونَ), the Messengers of Allah (أَنْبِيَاءُ اللهِ الْمُرْسَلُونَ), and the righteous servants of Allah (عِبَادُ اللهِ الصَّالِحُونَ) sought refuge, from the evil of what I saw in my vision (رُؤْيَاي).' And let him recite al-Hamd (الْحَمْد), al-Mu'awwidhatayn (الْمُعَوِّذَتَيْن), and Qul Huwa Allahu Ahad (قُلْ هُوَ اللهُ أَحَد), and let him spit lightly (يَتْفُل) to his left three times, for what he saw will not harm him." Then Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed to His Messenger: ** إِنَّمَا النَّجْوى مِنَ الشَّيْطانِ ** (Secret counsels are only from Satan), the Ayah.

And in al-Kafi, narrated from Him (peace be upon him), He said: If a man among you sees something he dislikes in his sleep, let him turn over from the side he was sleeping on, and let him say: ** إِنَّمَا النَّجْوى مِنَ الشَّيْطانِ لِيَحْزُنَ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَلَيْسَ بِضارِّهِمْ شَيْئاً إِلَّا بِإِذْنِ اللهِ ** (Secret counsels are only from Satan, so that he may grieve those who have faith, but they cannot harm them in the least, except by Allah’s leave). Then let him say: 'I seek refuge in that by which the near-stationed angels of Allah (مَلَائِكَةُ اللهِ الْمُقَرَّبُونَ), His Messengers (أَنْبِيَاؤُهُ الْمُرْسَلُونَ), and His righteous servants (عِبَادُهُ الصَّالِحُونَ) sought refuge, from the evil of what I saw and from the evil of the accursed Satan (الشَّيْطَانِ الرَّجِيمِ).'

(١١) ** يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِذا قِيلَ لَكُمْ تَفَسَّحُوا فِي الْمَجالِسِ ** (11) O you who have faith! When you are told, 'Make room in the assemblies,' Make space (توسّعوا) in them, and let some of you make room for others, derived from their saying, "Make room for me (افسح عنّي)," meaning, move aside (تنح). It was said that they used to crowd together (يتضامّون) in the assembly of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, competing for closeness to him and eager to hear his speech. And it was read (وقرئ) as في المجلس (in the assembly, singular).

فَافْسَحُوا يَفْسَحِ اللهُ لَكُمْ ** then make room; Allah will make room for you. In what you desire to be widened for you, such as place, provision (الرّزق), the breast/heart (الصدر), and other things.

وَإِذا قِيلَ انْشُزُوا ** And when you are told, 'Rise up,' Rise up (انهضوا) for the widening/making space (للتوسعة).

فَانْشُزُوا ** then rise up. And it was read (وقرئ) with the dammah on the shīn (بضمّ الشّين) in both (instances of انْشُزُوا). Al-Qummi said: When the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله entered the mosque, the people would stand up for him. So Allah forbade them from standing up for Him. Thus, He said: تَفَسَّحُوا (Make room), meaning: make space for Him in the assembly. وَإِذا قِيلَ انْشُزُوا فَانْشُزُوا (And when you are told, 'Rise up,' then rise up), meaning: if He says, "Stand up (قوموا)," then stand up (فقوموا).

** يَرْفَعِ اللهُ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا مِنْكُمْ ** Allah will raise in rank those of you who have faith Through victory (بالنصر) and good mention (حسن الذكر) in this world, and by sheltering them in the chambers of the Gardens (غرف الجنّات) in the Hereafter.

وَالَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْعِلْمَ دَرَجاتٍ ** and those who have been given knowledge, by [many] degrees. And He raises the scholars (العلماء) among them specifically with an increase in elevation (مزيد رفعة).

( A Narrative Discussion )

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: The superiority (فضل) of the scholar (العالم) over the martyr (الشهيد) is one degree (درجة), and the superiority of the martyr over the worshipper (العابد) is one degree, and the superiority of the Prophet over the scholar is one degree. And the superiority of the Quran over all other speech is like the superiority of Allah over His creation. And the superiority of the scholar over all other people is like My superiority over the lowest among them.

In al-Jawami', narrated from Him صلى الله عليه وآله: The superiority of the scholar over the worshipper is like the superiority of the moon on the night of the full moon (ليلة البدر) over all other stars (الكواكب).

And narrated from Him صلى الله عليه وآله: Between the scholar and the worshipper are one hundred degrees (مائة درجة). Between every two degrees is the distance covered by a trained, swift horse (الجواد المضمر) running for seventy years.

And narrated from Him صلى الله عليه وآله: Three groups will intercede (تشفع) on the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة): the Prophets, then the scholars, then the martyrs.

In al-Faqih, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام: When the Day of Resurrection comes, Allah will gather the people on one plain (صعيد واحد), and the Scales (الموازين) will be set up. The blood of the martyrs (دماء الشهداء) will be weighed against the ink of the scholars (مداد العلماء), and the ink of the scholars will outweigh the blood of the martyrs.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir عليه السلام: A scholar (عالم) whose knowledge is beneficial (ينتفع بعلمه) is superior to seventy thousand worshippers. And the narrations (الأخبار) concerning this meaning are too numerous to count (أكثر من أن تحصى).

وَاللهُ بِما تَعْمَلُونَ خَبِيرٌ ** and Allah is well aware of what you do. A threat (تهديد) to whoever does not comply with the command (الامر) and dislikes it (واستكرهه).

** يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِذا ناجَيْتُمُ الرَّسُولَ فَقَدِّمُوا بَيْنَ يَدَيْ نَجْواكُمْ صَدَقَةً ** (12) O you who have faith! When you secretly consult the Apostle, offer a charity before your secret consultation.

So offer charity before it (قُدَّامَهَا)—this is borrowed (مُسْتَعَار) from one who has two hands (مِمَّنْ لَهُ يَدَان). And in this command is the magnification (تَعْظِيم) of the Messenger, benefiting the poor (إِنْفَاع الْفُقَرَاء), the prohibition against excessive questioning (النَّهْي عَنِ الْإِفْرَاط فِي السُّؤَال), and the distinction (الْمَيْز) between the sincere (الْمُخْلِص) and the hypocrite (الْمُنَافِق), and the lover of the Hereafter (مُحِبّ الْآخِرَة) and the lover of the world (مُحِبّ الدُّنْيَا).

Al-Qummi said: If you ask the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله a need, then offer charity before your need, so that it may be more effective in fulfilling your needs (لِيَكُونَ أَقْضَى لِحَوَائِجِكُمْ). And no one did that except the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام, for he offered a dinar (دِينَار) in charity and secretly consulted the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله ten times (عَشْر نَجَوَات).

And narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام: He was asked about this Ayah, and He said: 'Ali ibn Abi Talib عليه السلام offered charity before his secret consultation, then His saying abrogated it: **أَأَشْفَقْتُمْ أَنْ تُقَدِّمُوا ** (Are you afraid to offer) the Ayah.

And with his chain of narration to Mujahid, he said: 'Ali عليه السلام said: "Indeed, there is an Ayah in the Book of Allah that no one before me acted upon, nor will anyone after me act upon it: the Ayah of the secret consultation (آيَة النَّجْوَى). I had a dinar (دِينَار), so I sold it for ten dirhams (دَرَاهِم), and I began offering a dirham (دِرْهَمًا) before every secret consultation I held with the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله." He said: Then His saying abrogated it: أَأَشْفَقْتُمْ ** (Are you afraid) up to His saying: خَبِيرٌ بِما تَعْمَلُونَ.** (is aware of what you do.)

And in al-Khisal, narrated from Him عليه السلام, in His argumentation against Abu Bakr, He said: "I adjure you by Allah (فَأَنْشُدُكَ بِاللَّهِ): Are you the one who offered charity before his secret consultation with the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله, and then consulted Him, while Allah the Almighty admonished a people and said: أَأَشْفَقْتُمْ ** (Are you afraid) the Ayah, or was it me?" He replied: "Rather, it was you." That is, that charity (ذَلِكَ التَّصَدُّق) خَيْرٌ لَكُمْ وَأَطْهَرُ ** (is better for you and purer) for your souls than adornment (الزِّينَة) and the love of wealth (حُبّ الْمَال). فَإِنْ لَمْ تَجِدُوا فَإِنَّ اللهَ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ ** (But if you do not find any, then Allah is indeed all-forgiving, all-merciful) for the one who does not find [anything], since He granted him a concession (رُخِّصَ لَهُ) to consult secretly without offering charity.

(١٣) أَأَشْفَقْتُمْ أَنْ تُقَدِّمُوا بَيْنَ يَدَيْ نَجْواكُمْ صَدَقاتٍ ** (Are you afraid to offer charities before your private talks?) Did you fear poverty from offering the charity, or did you fear the offering due to what Satan promises you concerning it, namely poverty? And the pluralization of صدقات (charities) is due to the plurality of those being addressed, or due to the frequency of the private talks (التناجي). ** فَإِذْ لَمْ تَفْعَلُوا وَتابَ اللهُ عَلَيْكُمْ ** (So when you did not do it, and Allah turned to you [in mercy]) by granting you a concession (رخّص لكم) not to do it.

In al-Khisal, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) regarding this verse: "Can repentance (التوبة) be except for a sin (ذنب)?"

** فَأَقِيمُوا الصَّلاةَ وَآتُوا الزَّكاةَ ** (maintain the prayer and give the zakat) And do not neglect (تفرّطوا) their performance. ** وَأَطِيعُوا اللهَ وَرَسُولَهُ ** (and obey Allah and His Messenger) in all other affairs, perhaps this will compensate for your negligence (تفريطكم) in that. ** وَاللهُ خَبِيرٌ بِما تَعْمَلُونَ ** (and Allah is well aware of what you do.) outwardly and inwardly.

** (١٤) أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَى الَّذِينَ تَوَلَّوْا ** (Do you not regard those who befriended) and took as allies ** قَوْماً غَضِبَ اللهُ عَلَيْهِمْ ** (a people with whom Allah is wrathful)—meaning the Jews— ما هُمْ مِنْكُمْ وَلا مِنْهُمْ ** (They are neither of you, nor of them), because they are hypocrites (منافقون), wavering (مذبذبون) between that. وَيَحْلِفُونَ عَلَى الْكَذِبِ وَهُمْ يَعْلَمُونَ (and swear falsely, while they know) that what is sworn upon is a lie, like one who swears the oath of al-Ghamūs (الغموس).

(١٥) أَعَدَّ اللهُ لَهُمْ عَذاباً شَدِيداً إِنَّهُمْ ساءَ ما كانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ. (15) Allah has prepared for them a severe punishment. Evil indeed is what they have been doing. (١٦) اتَّخَذُوا أَيْمانَهُمْ جُنَّةً a shield (وقاية) against their blood and their wealth فَصَدُّوا عَنْ سَبِيلِ اللهِ So they averted the people from the religion of Allah (عَن دين الله) during their state of security (في خلال امنهم) through incitement (بالتحريش) and discouragement (والتّثبيط) فَلَهُمْ عَذابٌ مُهِينٌ. (16) They have taken their oaths as a shield, and thus they bar [people] from the way of Allah. So there is a humiliating punishment for them. (١٧) لَنْ تُغْنِيَ عَنْهُمْ أَمْوالُهُمْ وَلا أَوْلادُهُمْ مِنَ اللهِ شَيْئاً أُولئِكَ أَصْحابُ النَّارِ هُمْ فِيها خالِدُونَ And its like has preceded. (17) Neither their wealth nor their children will avail them anything against Allah. They are the inmates of the Fire, and they will remain in it forever. (١٨) يَوْمَ يَبْعَثُهُمُ اللهُ جَمِيعاً فَيَحْلِفُونَ لَهُ meaning, to Allah the Almighty كَما يَحْلِفُونَ لَكُمْ in the world (في الدّنيا) وَيَحْسَبُونَ أَنَّهُمْ عَلى شَيْءٍ since hypocrisy (النّفاق) has become entrenched (تمكنّ) in their souls (نفوسهم) such that it is imagined (يخيّل إليهم) by them in the Hereafter (في الآخرة) that false oaths (الايمان الكاذبة) will promote (تروّج) the lie against Allah, just as they promote it against you in the world (في الدّنيا) أَلا إِنَّهُمْ هُمُ الْكاذِبُونَ those who have reached the extreme limit (الغاية) in lying (في الكذب), since they lie to the Knower of the Unseen and the Visible (عالم الغيب والشّهادة) and swear oaths to Him. (18) The day Allah raises them all, they will swear to Him, just as they swear to you, and suppose that they have some standing. Look! They are indeed the liars.

(١٩)** اسْتَحْوَذَ عَلَيْهِمُ الشَّيْطانُ ** (19) The devil has gained mastery over them gained dominance over them ** فَأَنْساهُمْ ذِكْرَ اللهِ ** and made them forget the remembrance of Allah. They do not remember Him with their hearts nor with their tongues ** أُولئِكَ حِزْبُ الشَّيْطانِ ** They are the devil’s gang. his soldiers and his followers ** أَلا إِنَّ حِزْبَ الشَّيْطانِ هُمُ الْخاسِرُونَ ** Look! The devil’s gang—it is they who are the losers. because they have missed out on eternal bliss for themselves and exposed themselves to everlasting torment.

Al-Qummi said: It was revealed concerning the Second (الثّاني) because the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله passed by him while he was sitting with a man from the Jews, writing down news about the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله. So, Allah the Almighty revealed the Ayah: أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَى الَّذِينَ تَوَلَّوْا (Have you not regarded those who befriended) [the rest of] the Ayah. Then the Second (الثاني) came to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said to him: "I saw you writing from the Jews, and Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, has forbidden that." He said: "O Messenger of Allah, I wrote from him what is in the التوراة (Torah) concerning your description." And he proceeded to read that to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله while He صلى الله عليه وآله was angry. A man from the الأنصار (Helpers) said to him: "Woe to you! Do you not see the anger of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله upon you?" He said: "I seek refuge in Allah from the wrath of Allah and the wrath of His Messenger. I only wrote that because of the good I found therein concerning you." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله then said: "O so-and-so, if Moses son of Imran were standing among them, and you came to him, turning away from what I have brought, you would be a disbeliever in what I have brought." And this is His saying: اتَّخَذُوا أَيْمانَهُمْ جُنَّةً (They have made their oaths a shield), meaning: shields (احجاباً) between them and the disbelievers, and their oaths are an affirmation by the tongue, out of fear of the sword and the lifting of the الجزية (Jizyah tax). And His saying: ** يَوْمَ يَبْعَثُهُمُ اللهُ جَمِيعاً فَيَحْلِفُونَ لَهُ كَما يَحْلِفُونَ لَكُمْ. (On the day when Allah raises them all, they will swear to Him just as they swear to you.) He said: When the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيمة) comes, Allah will gather those who usurped the right of the Family of Muhammad (آل محمّد). He will present their deeds to them, and they will swear to Him that they did not know anything about them, just as they swore to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله in this world when they swore not to restore the الولاية (guardianship/authority) to بنى هاشم (Banu Hashim), and when they intended to kill the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله at العقبة (Al-Aqabah). When Allah informed His Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله and told him, they swore that they had not said that and had not intended it, at the time when Allah revealed to His Messenger: ** يَحْلِفُونَ بِاللهِ ما قالُوا وَلَقَدْ قالُوا كَلِمَةَ الْكُفْرِ وَكَفَرُوا بَعْدَ إِسْلامِهِمْ وَهَمُّوا بِما لَمْ يَنالُوا وَما نَقَمُوا إِلَّا أَنْ أَغْناهُمُ اللهُ وَرَسُولُهُ مِنْ فَضْلِهِ فَإِنْ يَتُوبُوا يَكُ خَيْراً لَهُمْ ** (They swear by Allah that they did not say [it], but they certainly said the word of unfaith, and they became faithless after their submission, and they intended what they did not achieve. And they were resentful for no other reason than that Allah and His Messenger had enriched them out of His grace. So, should they repent, it will be better for them.) He said: When Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, presents that to them on the Day of Resurrection (القيامة), they will deny it and swear to Him, just as they swore to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله. And that is His saying: يَوْمَ يَبْعَثُهُمُ اللهُ جَمِيعاً (On the day when Allah raises them all) [the rest of] the Ayah. And another narrative concerning this has already preceded in Surat Ya Sin and Ha Mim As-Sajdah.

(٢٠) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يُحَادُّونَ اللهَ وَرَسُولَهُ أُولئِكَ فِي الْأَذَلِّينَ ** (20) Indeed, those who oppose Allah and His Apostle—they are among the most abased. [They are] among the group of those who are the most abased of Allah's creation. (٢١) كَتَبَ اللهُ ** [Allah] decreed in al-Lawh (the Tablet) ** لَأَغْلِبَنَّ أَنَا وَرُسُلِي ** by means of the proof (الحجّة) ** إِنَّ اللهَ قَوِيٌ ** over the victory of His prophets ** عَزِيزٌ ** Whom nothing can overcome regarding His will. (21) Allah has decreed: 'I shall surely overcome, I and My apostles.' Indeed, Allah is All-strong, All-mighty. In al-Majma', it is narrated: When the Muslims saw the villages that Allah was opening up for them, they said: "Allah will surely open up Rome (الرّوم) and Persia (فارس) for us." The hypocrites (المنافقون) then said: "Do you think that Persia (فارس) and Rome (الرّوم) are like some of the villages you have overcome?" So Allah sent down this verse.

(٢٢) ** لا تَجِدُ قَوْماً يُؤْمِنُونَ بِاللهِ وَالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ يُوادُّونَ مَنْ حَادَّ اللهَ وَرَسُولَهُ وَلَوْ كانُوا آباءَهُمْ أَوْ أَبْناءَهُمْ أَوْ إِخْوانَهُمْ أَوْ عَشِيرَتَهُمْ ** (22) You will not find any people who believe in Allah and the Last Day befriending those who oppose Allah and His Apostle, even though they were their fathers, or their sons, or their brothers, or their kinsmen. Even if those who oppose were the closest people to them. أُولئِكَ ** (meaning: those who did not befriend them) كَتَبَ فِي قُلُوبِهِمُ الْإِيمانَ ** (meaning: He established it therein) **وَأَيَّدَهُمْ بِرُوحٍ مِنْهُ ** (meaning: from His presence).

In al-Kafi, narrated from both of them (peace be upon them): It is faith (الإيمان).

And narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): There is no believer except that his heart has two ears within his chest: one ear into which الوسواس الخنّاس (the lurking whisperer) breathes, and one ear into which the angel breathes. So Allah strengthens the believer through the angel. That is His saying: and strengthened them with a spirit from Him.

And narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him): Indeed, Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, strengthened the believer with a spirit from Him which is present with him at every time he acts virtuously and is pious, and which is absent from him at every time he sins and transgresses. It is with him, trembling with joy when he acts virtuously, and sinking into the dust (الثَّرَى) when he acts wrongly. So, O servants of Allah, safeguard His blessings by reforming your souls, that you may increase in certainty (يَقِينًا) and gain a precious treasure. May Allah have mercy upon a man who intended good and acted upon it, or intended evil and restrained himself from it. Then he (peace be upon him) said: We strengthen the spirit through obedience to Allah and working for Him.

And narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding the saying of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "When a man commits fornication, the spirit of faith (روح الإيمان) departs from him." He (peace be upon him) said: It is His saying: وَأَيَّدَهُمْ بِرُوحٍ مِنْهُ —that is the one which departs from him. ** وَيُدْخِلُهُمْ جَنَّاتٍ تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهارُ خالِدِينَ فِيها رَضِيَ اللهُ عَنْهُمْ ** (meaning: due to their obedience) وَرَضُوا عَنْهُ ** (meaning: with His decree and with the reward He promised them). أُولئِكَ حِزْبُ اللهِ ** (meaning: His soldiers and the helpers of His religion.) أَلا إِنَّ حِزْبَ اللهِ هُمُ الْمُفْلِحُونَ ** (meaning: the winners of the good of both abodes.) The reward for reciting this Surah has already preceded at the end of Surah al-Hadid.

سُورَة الْحشَر

Medinan. Its number of verses is twenty-four verses, by consensus (بالإِجْماع).* بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ*** (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)
